item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W13
item.description: This week, approximately 10 projects were advanced in parallel across three devices (TzJsDesktop / tianhe / DCC). Core achievements: gadget’s summarize (2930 lines → 8 modules + 72 tests) and research_scout (2934 lines → 7 sub-packages) both completed systematic refactoring, with a new natural-language paper search ask command added; TokenMonitor evolved from a macOS-exclusive tool into a cross-platform multi-device SSH cost tracking platform (including Windows-native UX, floating ball, ccusage integration, LiteLLM dynamic pricing, comprehensive security hardening, and multiple successful MSI/NSIS installer builds); Error Recovery Benchmark completed Pipeline 2 full end-to-end design and implementation plus Context Replay architecture refactoring (163 tests all passing); ccplan / cchypothesis / optimize and other Claude Code toolchain components received systematic upgrades. On the robotics research front: Pi0.5 full-task rollout evaluation was completed (revealing extreme divergence: Stack 96% vs PickPlace 6%), BOSS benchmark was engineered into production, and openvla-oft training scripts were created. MIHD spatial transcriptomics completed QueST protocol alignment and an 8-encoder benchmark framework was set up.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-29
item.description: Ran five projects in parallel across the tianhe cluster and TzJsDesktop throughout the day: Error Recovery Benchmark completed full Pipeline 2 implementation (163 tests passing) and E4 architecture refactor; OpenPI evaluation achieved 20x performance improvement; gadget added natural language paper search via the ask command; TokenMonitor expanded from single-machine to SSH multi-device cost analysis platform (with comprehensive security hardening and multiple critical bug fixes); LifeCopilot completed codebase documentation and established integration direction with openclaw.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-28
item.description: A full day of parallel work across two machines (tianhe and TzJsDesktop) on four projects: tianhe completed the Context Replay conceptual refactor and code fixes for error_recovery_benchmark, and resolved LIBERO/BOSS evaluation environment missing assets with zero-shot design analysis; TzJsDesktop completed the intelligent dual-track instrumentation architecture integration for the cchypothesis skill, plus four progressive bug fixes in the TokenMonitor app — Dashboard jitter, Rust compilation errors, broken frontend imports, and window anchor inversion.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-27
item.description: Unified logging and result-saving logic across robot evaluation scripts, fully designed and implemented the cchypothesis hypothesis-driven debugging skill, and definitively fixed the TokenMonitor window bottom-edge jitter through 5 rounds of iteration.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-26
item.description: Full-day parallel work across three machines — DCC HPC, Tianhe supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop: DCC completed MIHD-QueST cross-sample query protocol alignment and the 8-gene encoder benchmark framework; Tianhe completed Pi0.5 LoRA full-task rollout evaluation, BOSS environment migration, and systematic Error Recovery Benchmark refactoring; TzJsDesktop performed multiple rounds of ccplan skill upgrades (Prompt Calibration, multi-intent decomposition, Feature Guard) and a major TokenMonitor feature iteration (float ball full lifecycle, Windows native UX, code refactoring, ccusage integration).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-25
item.description: On the tianhe server, multiple users completed the full VLA/robotics research stack setup (Git migration, training scripts, data pipeline fixes, OpenPI evaluation client). On TzJsDesktop: modular refactor of research_scout.py, cross-platform adaptation of TokenMonitor with the first Windows .exe build, four-language expansion of the optimize skill, and fine-grained Claude Code toolchain configuration.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-24
item.description: TzJsDesktop completed the gadget summarize module refactor (2930 lines → 8 modules + 72 tests), upgraded the summarize skill to a six-chapter academic paper format, and fully fixed the ccplan toolchain. Also initiated TokenMonitor’s architectural overhaul from macOS-only to cross-platform with a ccusage MCP backend, including Phase A/E implementation kickoff. tianhe created the BOSS benchmark repo CLAUDE.md and identified 5 bugs in the LiPM trainer, though a ~6-hour morning outage due to API connection failures disrupted progress.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-23
item.description: Deployed 5 backlogged daily reports (resolved remote conflicts via force push). Refactored flat bugJournal into daily/weekly/monthly three-level subdirectories, updated 6 Python script paths, rebuilt deployment. Cleaned git repository: removed 1839+44 mistakenly tracked files via git rm –cached, changed .gitignore to wildcard verify-public*/, added Git Tracking Rules to CLAUDE.md. Implemented sync.py bootstrap subcommand via cchelper requirements analysis (–remote parameter solves the bootstrapping problem). Batch-generated W05-W08 historical weekly report JSON. Multiple CLAUDE.md updates (sync.py commands, site_staging.py documentation, SITE_OUTPUTS_DIR path constant, build/ artifact description).

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W12
item.description: This week spanned three devices — DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop — with deep, broad advances across two research tracks: robot manipulation and spatial transcriptomics. The Error Recovery Benchmark progressed from collection scheme design (RBG grouping with 329-demo budget) to architecture-level trajectory segmentation refactoring (InteractionSegmenter), reaching a final count of 1,627 training scenes (148 subtypes, +35%). MIHD spatial transcriptomics completed the full cross-section embedding alignment pipeline and established scGPT’s zero-shot superiority (100% hit rate vs. UNI2’s 71%). π₀.₅ completed the full training pipeline for the task completion detection head (loss≈0.253) and designed five conditioning strategies for Exp5–9. The gadget toolchain completed Research Profiler disambiguation architecture refactoring, a unified deploy staging architecture for the website, and upgraded all ECC agents to opus + max thinking. The week’s core breakthroughs centered on ‘finding and fixing architecture-level root causes’: three systemic issues — non-comparable per-section PCA coordinate spaces, multi-object target_object ambiguity, and Flax NNX inheritance vs. composition — were all fundamentally resolved.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-22
item.description: tianhe cluster completed three core bug fixes for the Error Recovery Benchmark (gripper polarity reversal, wrong_object filtering logic, drop_with_interaction injection strategy), growing training scenes from 1209 to 1627 (+35%), with deep root-cause analysis of three_piece_assembly phase detection regression and physical constraint attribution for 48 ungenerable cases; TzJsDesktop batch-archived W05–W12 historical weekly reports, fixed the gadget website deployment pipeline, and completed Hugo site navigation dropdown menu redesign and unified deploy staging architecture.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-21
item.description: Completed full-stack repairs on error_recovery_benchmark in the tianhe HPC cluster — from parameter tuning to architectural trajectory segmentation refactoring (1,222 training scenes, threading subtypes increased from 3 to 25) — while fixing critical gadget infrastructure bugs on TzJsDesktop and upgrading the entire ECC toolchain to opus + max thinking.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-20
item.description: Simultaneously advanced four workstreams across DCC and tianhe HPC clusters: fixed drop_in_transit false positives in the error recovery benchmark and generated 973 training scenes for 6 tasks in parallel with 32 workers (41 minutes); built a Pi0.5-base LoRA fine-tuning pipeline for a merged 6-task dataset (data conversion and norm stats complete, training interrupted by Slurm job expiry); fixed unfair comparison in STAIG hyperparameter sweep and established PCA+UNI/UNI2 baselines (ARI 0.47); and developed a scanpy analysis script for 10x Visium spatial transcriptomics.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-19
item.description: Today’s AI conversation summary

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-18
item.description: Advanced four parallel research tracks across DCC, tianhe, and local machines: on DCC, completed end-to-end delivery of MIHD cross-slice embedding alignment and batch effect evaluation; on tianhe, launched π₀.₅ task completion detection head training, fixed missing manip_progress recording in the VLA evaluation pipeline, completed LIBERO environment fixes, and designed Exp5-9 configurations; on local, implemented batch task status update intent for CalendarPro, resolving a core defect in high-frequency daily interactions.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-17
item.description: DCC: comprehensive code refactoring of the MIHD multimodal spatial transcriptomics project (/simplify fixed 9 issues) with documentation updates. tianhe: multiple researchers in parallel completed robobrain_pi task state machine bug fixes and training loss split monitoring, LIBERO test suite integration confirmation, error recovery benchmark 2920-scene data quality verification, CALVIN format conversion script fix, GPU monitor deduplication and keyboard navigation addition, and RoboCasa MimicGen pretrained data download upstream root cause confirmation.

item.title: Enes Eken — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Enes Eken: Enes Eken’s academic career follows a clear three-phase trajectory. In the first phase (2014–2018), he focused deeply on STT-RAM reliability design, building a solid technical foundation around read/write reliability enhancement, variation-aware simulation tools, and error-correction coding schemes. His most-cited papers all come from this period.

item.title: Fan Chen — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Fan Chen: The “Fan Chen” in this dataset most likely represents merged records from multiple researchers sharing the same name, rather than the complete trajectory of a single scholar. Based on the paper content, at least two distinctly different researchers can be identified: (1) a researcher with a long-term focus on analytical chemistry/food chemistry, spanning 1992–2022, evolving from early forensic gas chromatography to ionic li…

item.title: Hsin-Pai Cheng — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Hsin-Pai Cheng: Hsin-Pai Cheng is a researcher specializing in efficient AI systems and edge deployment. His academic trajectory falls into three distinct phases. From 2016 to 2018, he used the IBM TrueNorth neuromorphic platform as an entry point to study neural network quantization and adversarial robustness under hardware constraints, building a systematic understanding of ‘deploying AI under compute limitations’

item.title: Ismail Bayram — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Ismail Bayram: Ismail Bayram is a pragmatic Turkish researcher in agricultural/veterinary science, with a career spanning 1999–2025. His core expertise lies in the nutritional science of poultry (quail, broilers, laying hens) and ruminants (sheep, buffalo, dairy cows). His academic trajectory began with basic feed additives (probiotics, vitamins, fishmeal substitutes

item.title: Jiachen Mao — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Jiachen Mao: Jiachen Mao’s academic trajectory is exceptionally rare, marked by dramatic cross-domain pivots that resist characterization by conventional ’established leader / rising star’ frameworks. From 2015 to 2018, their primary contributions were in building energy simulation and urban microclimate, publishing multiple highly cited papers on high-rise ventilation, energy model calibration, and urban heat island analysis — establishing an early academic reputation

item.title: Jie Guo — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Jie Guo: A critical issue must be noted upfront — based on the actual content of the paper list, this is not a single researcher’s academic trajectory, but rather the result of incorrectly aggregated data from multiple researchers sharing the same name (郭杰/郭洁). The highly-cited papers from 2000–2005 (with over 2,000 cumulative citations) belong to a public health researcher focused on adolescent substance abuse and social development.

item.title: Jun Lu — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Jun Lu: Jun Lu’s academic trajectory shows a clear two-phase evolution. The first phase (1989–2013) focused on numerical electromagnetic field computation, particularly the Harmonic Balance Finite-Element Method (HB-FEM) and high-frequency planar transformer design, establishing a solid methodological foundation in nonlinear magnetic field analysis. A pivotal transition occurred around 2013–2015

item.title: Kent W. Nixon — 研究者分析报告
item.description: Kent W. Nixon — 早期研究者

item.title: L. Teng — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: L. Teng: L. Teng’s academic trajectory is highly fragmented, with publications spanning completely unrelated fields including glioma treatment, cardiac stem cells, NADPH oxidase vascular biology, marine model organism immunology, and GaAs quantum well spin relaxation — strongly suggesting this name aggregates multiple distinct researchers (name disambiguation problem). Focusing on the core research thread,

item.title: Linghao Song — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Linghao Song: Linghao Song is a computer architecture researcher whose work consistently centers on in/near-memory computing. In 2017, his ReRAM-based graph processing accelerator GraphR won a Best Paper Award, establishing his standing in

item.title: Nathan Inkawhich — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Nathan Inkawhich: Nathan Inkawhich is a researcher working at the intersection of adversarial machine learning and SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) remote sensing recognition. Since 2017, he has developed in parallel along two tracks — defense/remote sensing applications and deep learning security. His academic breakthrough came in 2019-2020, with consecutive publications at CVPR, NeurIPS (two papers)

item.title: Wu Lin — Researcher Profile Analysis
item.description: Wu Lin: A critical note: the publication list suffers from severe name-disambiguation issues. ‘Wu Lin’ is a common Chinese name, and the database has aggregated work from at least 5 distinct researchers across different fields: ① a plant-soil ecologist (replant disease/rhizosphere microbiome, 2001–2025, forming the core profile); ② a computer vision researcher (person re-identification, 2

item.title: Xiangliu Chen — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Xiangliu Chen — Early-Stage Researcher

item.title: Xiaoxiao Liu — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Xiaoxiao Liu: It should first be noted that this paper list has a clear name disambiguation issue. The papers span completely unrelated fields including cancer biology, GPU architecture, plant immunity, hyperspectral image processing, genomics, and LLM prompt engineering, clearly aggregated from multiple researchers sharing the same name. Base…

item.title: Xiongfei Yu — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Xiongfei Yu: The academic trajectory of Xiongfei Yu exhibits a rare multi-phase cross-disciplinary development pattern, likely representing the aggregated works of multiple researchers sharing the same name. The first phase (1996–2004) centered on petroleum engineering in China, focusing on gas condensate prediction in gas reservoirs, CO₂ injection for enhanced oil recovery (EOR), and reservoir screening for gas injection, with a distinctly

item.title: Yan Yang — 研究者分析报告
item.description: Yan Yang — 早期研究者

item.title: Yanyan Chen — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Yanyan Chen: Warning: This “Yanyan Chen” academic profile has a severe Name Disambiguation Failure — Semantic Scholar has very likely merged dozens of researchers sharing the name “Yanyan Chen” into a single profile

item.title: Yingying Huang — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Yingying Huang: Based on the publication list, this ‘Yingying Huang’ is most likely the result of merged data from multiple namesake researchers rather than a single researcher’s academic trajectory. Publications span chemical biology (DNA-small molecule interactions, natural products), plasma physics (GS reconstruction), condensed matter physics (VO₂ phase transitions), surface physics, machine learning/B

item.title: Yiran Chen — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Yiran Chen: Yiran Chen’s academic trajectory shows three distinct transitions. Phase one (2003-2007) focused on power optimization in traditional computer architecture, exemplified by deterministic clock gating (DCG), representing solid grounding in classical VLSI design. Phase two (2008-2015) was the pivotal period in which he became a defining figure in the emerging memory

item.title: Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Analysis Report
item.description: Yongfeng Ding: Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher at the very beginning of their academic career, having started publishing in 2023 with only 4 papers to date. They do not yet exhibit the hallmarks of a field leader, but have demonstrated notable originality in composite material microstructure quantification. Their core contribution centers on the ‘degree of randomness’ metric proposed in 2023.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16
item.description: Parallel progress across three machines — DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop: DCC completed cross-section Foundation Model validation for spatial transcriptomics (scGPT 100% hit rate) and bilingual documentation; tianhe finished the 329-demo VLA error recovery collection plan, full pipeline implementation, fixed the CompositeBodyObject transform bug, and launched batch error scene generation; TzJsDesktop processed 20+ researcher academic profiles in bulk and ran two rounds of Research Profiler refactoring, ultimately fixing systematic S2 disambiguation failures and achieving correct h-index recognition for prominent professors.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W11
item.description: This week, six parallel workstreams advanced across three machines (DCC, tianhe, TzJsDesktop): ①MIHD spatial transcriptomics uncovered a fundamental methodological flaw in cross-sample embedding (per-section independent processing causes incomparable feature spaces) and initiated a fix; ②ErrorRecoveryBenchmark scaled from bug fixes to 13 skills/29 subtypes, solved the Drop skill object-not-falling issue, exposed online quota architecture limitations, and established offline injection as the new direction; ③VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 achieved end-to-end progress from environment setup and training performance optimization (JAX version alignment +33% speedup) to new data variable collection and auxiliary task experiments; ④gadget toolchain completed an architectural upgrade with MCP Server + common/ shared package + unified output directory, and the research profiler achieved homepage-first student discovery; ⑤CalendarPro completed 7-phase comprehensive optimization with all 230 tests passing and token consumption reduced by 40–60%; ⑥gadget research toolchain integrated citation graph analysis and produced deep profiles for 7 embodied AI researchers.

item.title: Haiyong Wang — 研究者分析报告
item.description: Haiyong Wang — 早期研究者

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-15
item.description: Six parallel workstreams across three devices all day: DCC diagnosed the root cause of cross-sample embedding dual non-comparability in MIHD; tianhe completed ErrorRecoveryBenchmark v5 fixes for five failing skills, E2 semantic split (13 skills/29 subtypes), v4 archival, D0 scene generation, and v5.1 planning, while also fixing the full VLA progress prediction training pipeline and debugging UniVLA container compatibility; TzJsDesktop completed CalendarPro’s seven-phase comprehensive optimization (230 tests passing), gadget common/ package and outputs/ unification refactor, research profiler homepage-based student discovery with Hugo deployment, and research toolchain CLI consolidation (citation graph + three-backend LLM support).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-14
item.description: Improved GPU monitoring tool UX, completed four-camera visualization for 50 robot demonstration episodes, and designed and implemented a four-group manipulation progress prediction auxiliary task experiment framework in the pi05 VLA model

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-13
item.description: Added five new per-frame data variables to the RoboTwin Place Dual Shoes robot task and fixed two data collection quality bugs

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-12
item.description: Debugging multi-layered dependency issues in a VLA robot evaluation environment on the Tianhe server, while improving GPU monitor display logic

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-11
item.description: Completed a major MIHD output directory restructure and the 151673↔151508 cross-section RM-IDEAL benchmark on DCC; developed a K8s in-container GPU monitoring tool on tianhe, and systematically diagnosed and fixed the 33% performance bottleneck in pi05 VLA training vs. openpi (by aligning 6 key dependency versions including JAX)

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-10
item.description: Today’s AI conversation summary

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-09
item.description: Parallel progress across DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop: MIHD cross-modal enhancement module implementation and RM-IDEAL cross-sample evaluation, robot error recovery benchmark quota-based data generation (exposing online architecture limitations and establishing the offline injection approach), VLA robot framework engineering (UniVLA/Pi0/RoboTwin data pipelines), and upgrading gadget tools into AI Agent-callable MCP services with CalendarPro integration.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W10
item.description: This week, spanning three devices — DCC, Tianhe HPC, and TzJsDesktop — four parallel tracks advanced: spatial transcriptomics research, robot manipulation training/evaluation, AI personal assistant, and paper management. The MIHD project completed a full suite of scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% vs scGPT-only) and established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning achieved an overall 58.9% success rate, decisively outperforming BC-RNN (0%) and quantitatively validating VLA model superiority. CalendarPro underwent an architectural leap from reactive to proactive decision-making (321 tests passing), while uncovering and fixing critical integration bugs including BackgroundCoordinator never being started. gadget Research Scout was built from scratch to production-ready in a single day, implementing a two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipeline and generating 3 actionable research directions for the first time. error_recovery_benchmark completed full cleanup of 65 symlinks and built the MP4 visualization infrastructure for error scenarios. The most important engineering lessons of the week: passing tests ≠ system availability (the integration layer must be verified independently), and before designing agentic systems, proactively study the architectural patterns of mature comparable projects.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-08
item.description: Completed a large-scale error_recovery_benchmark refactor on the tianhe server (full cleanup of 65 symlinks + code path migration), developed multi-task error scenario MP4 visualization and a pre-error replay storage layer, advanced the VLA-RoboTwin critical region annotation system and Vulkan rendering fix, and locally added author search and several other features to gadget research_scout.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-07
item.description: Completed the full lifecycle build of the gadget Research Scout paper management system in a single day with first validation producing 3 research directions; advanced CalendarPro intent classification system root cause analysis and cross-project architecture spec design (introducing OpenClaw context window management model); and batch-filled three days of backlogged daily reports.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-06
item.description: Three devices progressing in parallel on the same day: DCC traced the normalization pipeline for ContraVAE and STHD spatial transcriptomics data; tianhe fixed three consecutive bugs in the RoboTwin evaluation pipeline (Vulkan rendering, frozen dataclass, duplicate path) and completed the Phoenix/FLARE training script suite covering all 9 tasks; TzJsDesktop wrote a 1674-line Chinese tutorial for CalendarPro and identified three root causes behind the Discord bot’s cross-message context loss.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05
item.description: Parallel progress across three machines (DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop) on four projects: DCC completed the MIHD multimodal spatial omics cross-sample benchmark and established a zero-shot narrative framework; tianhe deployed the zhaoganlong framework’s 9-task training pipeline, launched two training runs, fixed an Openpi-moe normalization issue, and advanced the Phoenix/FLARE codebase separation; TzJsDesktop upgraded CalendarPro into a personal AI chief-of-staff with autonomous perception and multi-agent orchestration capabilities (321 tests passing)

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04
item.description: DCC conducted an in-depth analysis of the QueST spatial transcriptomics paper, focusing on its cross-sample query and evaluation methodology; tianhe completed multiple project documentation updates (error recovery benchmark panorama summary.md + zhaoganlong training guide), diagnosed the root cause of BC-RNN zero success rate, and planned the an49 full-task training infrastructure.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-03
item.description: Completed cross-section RM-IDEAL benchmark implementation and methodology documentation on DCC; completed full evaluation of Pi0.5 LoRA (58.9% vs BC-RNN 0%) on the Tianhe supercomputing cluster, verified training weight sources (confirmed all use pi05_base), and resumed training for 9 tasks.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02
item.description: Completed the full scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiment suite and weekly report visualization on DCC, identified the key architectural fact that STAIG does not use the gene encoder; managed migration and restart of 9 Pi0.5 LoRA training jobs across two Tianhe nodes (an49/an53), fixed multiple critical issues in the eval pipeline (WebSocket timeout, JIT concurrency crash), conducted in-depth root cause analysis of the ~10% GPU utilization in VLA inference, and completed the design of a batched concurrent eval optimization plan.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W09
item.description: This week we ran two parallel workstreams across the DCC and Tianhe clusters: spatial transcriptomics (MIHD) and the robot Error Recovery Benchmark. On DCC, we fixed a critical scGPT weight-loading bug (average ARI improvement of 44.4%), extended the MIHD benchmark to Visium HD datasets, and completed a large-scale repository refactor (~250K lines of code). On Tianhe, we built the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline from scratch (9 tasks in parallel), identified and fixed the root cause of Pi0.5 evaluation achieving 0% success rate (task distribution mismatch), obtained key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation (learned policies achieve SR≈0% in error scenarios, justifying M15 LoRA fine-tuning), and successfully launched Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task parallel fine-tuning stably across 6×A800 GPUs.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-03-01
item.description: Parallel progress across four projects on DCC and tianhe servers: DCC completed VisiumHD three-annotation visualization and STAIG fusion multimodal run (Silhouette 0.343), and executed a 6-phase large-scale cleanup refactor of the MIHD repo (deleted ~250K lines of code); tianhe completed Pi0.5 9-task base model evaluation (4.2% SR), diagnosed and fixed missing Coffee BC-RNN object observation key, developed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (fixed 4-layer image bug, generated 11 scenarios), and successfully launched Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task parallel fine-tuning at 100% GPU utilization on 6×A800 after fixing 4 startup bugs.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28
item.description: Completed full-pipeline Visium HD extension for the MIHD project on DCC (8 files modified) and fixed a critical scGPT weight-loading bug (ARI +44.4%); on tianhe, built the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline from scratch and successfully launched 9 tasks in parallel training, while also completing the first Pi0.5 Phoenix evaluation deployment (7/9 tasks done).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-27
item.description: Completed storage analysis on the Tianhe HPC cluster, documented the Slurm GPU node request workflow, and confirmed M14 baseline evaluation results (Pi0/BC-RNN achieved near-zero success rates on error recovery scenarios)

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-26
item.description: Rewrote BC-RNN training configs to image mode on the HPC cluster and successfully launched 5-task parallel training. Extended the evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks, then identified and fixed the task distribution mismatch causing Pi0.5’s 0% success rate.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-23
item.description: Systematically optimized MIHD spatial transcriptomics fusion training on the DCC node (3x CPU acceleration + architecture decoupling + full-slide benchmarking + Vision Refine ablation experiments). Concurrently on the Tianhe cluster, completed MimicGen data preparation, fixed M14 three-way evaluation environment fingerprint crashes, and resolved Pi0.5 full fine-tuning OOM issues. Successfully brought Pi0.5 LoRA training (Job 46553) to stable operation.

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W08
item.description: This week centered on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project, completing a systematic survey of H&E Image-Only clustering methods (establishing ARI 0.11–0.16 literature baseline), implementing three self-supervised enhancement schemes (SCAN boosted ARI from 0.251 to 0.303, +20.6%), and building the Vision Refinement two-stage fusion framework. Simultaneously on the tianhe cluster: Error Recovery Benchmark (M14 evaluation infrastructure validated, full 649-scenario evaluation launched) and Phoenix pi0.5 reproduction data pipeline (18.4GB MimicGen dataset ingested, training config ready). Resolved multiple engineering blockers including STEGO NaN, double normalization bug, lerobot version conflict, and HuggingFace proxy issues. Pi0.5 OOM and visualize_scene.py video validation remain blocked for next week.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-22
item.description: DCC completed MIHD project cleanup and Vision Refinement two-stage fusion implementation with batch experiments launched; tianhe advanced Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II (M14 evaluation pipeline validation, Pi0.5 OOM diagnosis) and completed the Phoenix pi0.5 reproduction full data pipeline (9 MimicGen task datasets ingested at 18.4GB, training config ready).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-20
item.description: Ran targeted tests on STAIG fusion for the MIHD project, discovered a double-normalization bug in eval_scan_fusion.py, and introduced pipeline-level embedding caching into run_benchmark.py.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-19
item.description: In the MIHD project, completed a systematic literature review of H&E Image-Only clustering methods (establishing ARI 0.11–0.16 baselines and five root causes for Foundation Model failure), built four core technical documents, and implemented and validated three self-supervised clustering enhancement approaches (STEGO/BYOL+GAT/SCAN). SCAN improved image-only ARI from 0.251 to 0.303 (+20.6%).

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W07
item.description: This week spanned three parallel tracks: robotics simulation, bioinformatics, and toolchain work. error_recovery_benchmark hit a deep blocker in force injection debugging (30N had no visible effect on the OSC controller), revealing a fundamental issue with controller cancellation; MIHD completed the Chinese documentation for its enhancement plan; ccusage shipped GLM multi-model billing support with type/format checks passing; robobrain_pi confirmed the training pipeline is ready. This week exposed clear pattern-reuse failures in AI — repeating the same error (SSH missing cd commands) and making bad environment assumptions (proxy, pnpm) — while human insight at key decision points (extreme force testing, detecting local GPU, reusing existing pricing files) proved far more efficient.

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-14
item.description: Implemented force injection enhancements for a robotic arm error-recovery benchmark, but 30N still produces no visible perturbation in video — root cause unresolved

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-13
item.description: Translated the MIHD project enhancement plan document to Chinese and wrote it to a new file

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-12
item.description: Implemented and debugged GLM billing support for ccusage on tianhe; organized the Chinese version of the MIHD enhancement plan on DCC

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-10
item.description: Progress across three projects: finalized contributor documentation for the robotics benchmark project, organized robobrain_pi history and prepared SAC reinforcement learning training, and kicked off documentation updates for the gadget research module

item.title: Weekly Summary 2026-W06
item.description: This week (2026-02-06~07) work focused on two main tracks: first, a systematic root-cause analysis of the ARI performance gap between the MIHD project’s staig_fusion and the original STAIG (0.21 → target 0.56), identifying and quantifying five key implementation differences and completing the code-level strict-alignment override refactor; second, an engineering upgrade to the benchmark tool — fixing a bar chart rendering bug in reports, implementing an end-to-end GitHub Pages auto-publish pipeline, and adding interactive CLI upload functionality. Additionally, one TOEFL English speaking practice session was recorded (personal study).

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-07
item.description: Today’s AI Conversation Summary

item.title: Bug Journal 2026-02-06
item.description: Across DCC cluster and TzJsDesktop, systematically diagnosed and quantified the five root causes behind MIHD staig_fusion’s performance gap versus the original STAIG, while delivering the benchmark tool’s bar chart rendering fix, GitHub Pages automated publishing pipeline, and CLI interactive upload functionality.

item.title: LeetCode Daily Question 2025-12-05
item.description: Solution to 3432. Count Partitions with Even Sum Difference

item.title: Weekly Summary 2025-W40
item.description: This week (2025-W40) had only one active session on October 2nd, and that session remained in the initialization phase — the user opened the core tracking and segmentation files of the AutoSeg-SAM2 project but did not proceed to any substantive development work. No meaningful progress was made this week overall.

item.title: Bug Journal 2025-10-02

item.title: LLM Study
item.description:
Part ChatGPT 4o

ChatGPT 4o API demo

from openai import OpenAI
model_use = "gpt-4o-2024-08-06"
client = OpenAI(api_key="Your-API-key")
completion = client.beta.chat.completions.parse(
	model=model_use,
	messages=[
		{"role": "system", "content": "Extract the event information."},
		{"role": "user", "content": "Alice and Bob are going to a science fair on Friday."},
	],
)
event = completion.choices[0].message.parsed

Note: I tried to use model “gpt-4o” but failed.

How to create ChatGPT API Key

  1. Log in to openai
  2. Use the search bar to search “API keys”
  3. Create a new secret key (Shown only once, invisible after closing the tab)
  4. Go to billing to add some credit to the account

Part UniTox ChatGPT

Read from fda.gov

  1. Read the label of the drug we are interested in from a .csv file.

item.title: LeetCode Daily Question 2025-01-01
item.description: 1422. Maximum Score After Splitting a String

item.title: Random Ideas
item.description:
1. 笔记本的 RAM 在关闭屏幕后还耗电吗
  2. markdown 插入图片无法在网站上自动显示
  3. Random 中加一个 checkbox
  4. desktop video 英文版 √
  5. 孤波算法是什么
  6. desktop video 多语言切换 √
  7. 是什么成就了一个奢侈品？
  8. Desktop Video 锁屏界面播放
  9. M4 pro V.S. M3 Max

Zijia (Thomas) Tang

Email: zijia.tang@duke.edu | Phone: +1 (858) 340-5757

Interest Areas: Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, AI4Science, Bioinformatics

Education

Duke University | Aug 2024 – Present

  • Major: Computer Science (B.S.)
  • GPA: 4.0 / 4.0
  • Relevant Coursework: Applied Machine Learning, Advanced Algorithms, Linear Algebra

Selected Publications

FLARE: A Failure-Aware Framework for Autonomous Correction and Recovery in VLA Co-First Author | CVPR 2026

  • Addressed the “brittleness” of current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models where minor perturbations lead to catastrophic failure, aiming to endow robots with human-like resilience.
  • Proposed a novel Retry & Reset paradigm and designed a Perturbation & Bridging augmentation pipeline that systematically decouples robot pose from environment state to resolve In-Distribution (ID) errors.
  • Integrated Gemini-2.5-Pro as an offline failure analyst to mine object-centric “Reset” skills for Out-of-Distribution (OOD) recovery. Implemented using LoRA fine-tuning on Pi_0.5 backbones.
  • Achieved an 84.0% success rate across 9 contact-rich tasks in RoboMimic, outperforming previous SOTA by 26.2%.

scDrugMap: Benchmarking Large Foundation Models for Drug Response Prediction Co-Author | Published in Nature Communications (Impact Factor: 15.7)

item.title: Benchmark
item.description: Open the full HTML report
